element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player names pilot and chooses call sign before first mission
Player learns to coordinate with AI-controlled wingman
Missions can proceed if protagonist survives even if objectives fail

element_introduction_order.element: Multiple enemy ship types with varying capabilities
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player encounters progressively more capable enemy fighters
Success unlocks better ships; failure results in inferior craft
Wingmen can be killed permanently, impacting rest of campaign
If player fails objectives, missions shift to defensive nature
Final objective: lead strike on Kilrathi High Command starbase in Venice system
New Kilrathi weapon increases gravity 100x; mounted on Sivar-class dreadnought
Upgraded Dralthi II, Hhriss-class heavy fighter, Snakeir-class heavy carrier
Firekkans negotiating Confederation membership; tensions escalate

typical_tension_triggers: Introduction of new enemy weapon types
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Campaign-based branching rhythm: success builds momentum with better ships and offensive strikes; failure creates sustained tension with defensive missions and inferior equipment. Expansion packs introduce escalating threats (graviton weapon, advanced ships) that spike difficulty.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory: pilot customization, basic fighter controls, single wingman support, manageable enemy encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Progressive challenge: multi-objective complexity increases, multiple enemy ship types, wingman permanent death risk, branching paths diverge based on performance
difficulty_curve.late_game: Success path: high-stakes strike missions against major targets (Kilrathi High Command). Failure path: sustained defensive operations with refugee evacuations, carrier retreat.
notable_spikes: Introduction of Kilrathi High Command strike (success path endpoint)
Graviton weapon revelation and Sivar-class dreadnought encounter (Secret Missions 1)
Massive Kilrathi battle group with advanced ships (Secret Missions 2)

notable_level_patterns.description: Performance-based mission progression where player success/failure determines future mission difficulty, ship quality, and strategic direction. Winning path leads to offensive operations; losing path forces defensive retreat.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Core campaign missions 1-30+ depending on performance

notable_level_patterns.description: Campaign concludes in either offensive victory (strike on Venice system Kilrathi High Command) or defensive retreat (Claw forced to evacuate). Winning path canonically continues into expansions and sequels.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Campaign finale varies by performance track

notable_level_patterns.description: Secret Missions introduce progressively deadlier Kilrathi assets: graviton weapon technology (SM1), then advanced fighter and carrier classes (SM2), with military/diplomatic consequences.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Secret Missions 1: Sivar dreadnought strike. Secret Missions 2: Firekka system defense against battle group

notable_level_patterns.description: Player develops reliance on AI wingman support throughout campaign; wingmen can die permanently, creating persistent campaign consequences and forcing adaptation.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any mission where wingman combat engagement occurs
